method: Live-app instrumentation via chrome-devtools: captured AudioContext via initScript hook (zero source edits), drove splash->title->in-system flow, sampled (audioContext.currentTime, performance.now()) every 1s for 60s during real well-dipper sim load.

runs.scenario: title-screen-with-title-music + sim active (camera auto-rotate, sky shader)

stats.shape: bounded oscillation around zero, no monotone growth

runs.scenario: in-system-autopilot-screensaver + synthetic tone, post-warmup, full sim load

stats.shape: bounded with periodic ~12-second buffer-scheduling dips at i=15,27,39,52; not monotone
stats.periodicity_note: ~12-second period dips of ~-11ms drift recover within 3 samples — consistent with audio output device buffer re-sync (hardware/driver behavior, NOT sim-induced)

verdict_reasoning: Both runs show: (1) slope near zero (|slope| < 0.1 ms/sec — well under 1 ms/sec threshold), (2) end-to-end drift bounded under 5 ms over 60 s, (3) no monotone-growth signature. Per-sample peaks of ~10-12 ms are: (a) setInterval scheduling jitter when JS thread is busy with sim work, and (b) audio output device buffer re-sync events (visible as periodic ~12-second dips in run 2). Neither is sim-induced audio clock motion. Audio clock advances at real-time rate independent of sim load, confirming audit-list ruling at docs/refactor-audits/fixed-timestep-migration-call-sites.md sec Audio clock domain.
brief_threshold_note: Brief AC #19 specifies +/- 2 ms threshold. Literal reading fails because per-sample peaks reach ~12 ms — but those peaks are interval-jitter and hardware-buffer artifacts, not audio drift. Recommended interpretation: slope-based criterion (|slope| <= 1 ms/sec, no monotone shape) — surface to Max for ratification per the brief's open-question framing for fuzzy gates. The brief itself acknowledges this kind of metric needs qualitative judgment in the AC #5 note.
